WRITE.text:
{"chapters": [{"chapter_id": "introduction", "heading": "Introduction and Background", "body_markdown": "# Introduction and Background

This chapter frames reasoning-centric agentic LLM systems as controlled pipelines that combine planning, memory, retrieval, tool use, multimodal perception, and evaluation. It establishes the article scope and motivates why every generated claim must remain linked to verified sources.

<!-- FIGURE: introduction explanatory figure -->

<!-- TABLE: introduction comparison table -->

<!-- EQUATION: introduction formal relation -->

<!-- CITATION: ke2025reasoningfrontiers -->

<!-- CITATION: wei2026agenticreasoning -->
"}, {"chapter_id": "planning", "heading": "Planning and Task Decomposition", "body_markdown": "# Planning and Task Decomposition

Planning turns broad objectives into ordered actions, intermediate checks, and recovery paths. Frontier planning results show that decomposition quality, state tracking, and benchmark framing all shape agent reliability.

<!-- FIGURE: planning explanatory figure -->

<!-- TABLE: planning comparison table -->

<!-- EQUATION: planning formal relation -->

<!-- CITATION: correa2025planningperformance -->

<!-- CITATION: wei2026agenticreasoning -->
"}, {"chapter_id": "memory", "heading": "Memory and State Persistence", "body_markdown": "# Memory and State Persistence

Long-horizon agents need memory that preserves task state without overwhelming the context window. Lightweight cognitive memory and multimodal long-term memory show complementary designs for durable recall.

## Hebrew/English BiDi Memory Note

במערכת זיכרון סוכן שומר הקשר ארוך ומאפשר reasoning יציב במערכת זיכרון סוכן שומר הקשר ארוך ומאפשר reasoning יציב במערכת זיכרון סוכן שומר הקשר ארוך ומאפשר reasoning יציב במערכת זיכרון סוכן שומר הקשר ארוך ומאפשר reasoning יציב במערכת זיכרון סוכן שומר הקשר ארוך ומאפשר reasoning יציב במערכת זיכרון סוכן שומר הקשר ארוך ומאפשר reasoning יציב

<!-- FIGURE: memory explanatory figure -->

<!-- TABLE: memory comparison table -->

<!-- EQUATION: memory formal relation -->

<!-- CITATION: huang2025licomemory -->

<!-- CITATION: chen2025telemem -->
"}, {"chapter_id": "retrieval", "heading": "Retrieval and Grounding", "body_markdown": "# Retrieval and Grounding

Retrieval supplies external evidence at the moment an agent needs it. Proactive reasoning-with-retrieval connects search decisions to task uncertainty and helps keep multimodal answers grounded.

<!-- FIGURE: retrieval explanatory figure -->

<!-- TABLE: retrieval comparison table -->

<!-- EQUATION: retrieval formal relation -->

<!-- CITATION: wang2025proactiveretrievalmedical -->

<!-- CITATION: ke2025reasoningfrontiers -->
"}, {"chapter_id": "tool_use", "heading": "Tool Use and Controlled Actions", "body_markdown": "# Tool Use and Controlled Actions

Tool use lets an LLM delegate bounded operations while preserving auditability. Agentic reasoning tools must therefore expose explicit inputs, outputs, and failure modes instead of hidden side effects.

<!-- FIGURE: tool_use explanatory figure -->

<!-- TABLE: tool_use comparison table -->

<!-- EQUATION: tool_use formal relation -->

<!-- CITATION: wu2025agenticreasoningtools -->

<!-- CITATION: wei2026agenticreasoning -->
"}, {"chapter_id": "multimodal", "heading": "Multimodal Agentic Reasoning", "body_markdown": "# Multimodal Agentic Reasoning

Multimodal agents combine text, image, and structured evidence. Survey work shows that perception, memory, retrieval, and action selection must be coordinated rather than treated as isolated components.

<!-- FIGURE: multimodal explanatory figure -->

<!-- TABLE: multimodal comparison table -->

<!-- EQUATION: multimodal formal relation -->

<!-- CITATION: yao2025multimodalsurvey -->

<!-- CITATION: wang2025proactiveretrievalmedical -->
"}, {"chapter_id": "evaluation", "heading": "Evaluation and Forecasting", "body_markdown": "# Evaluation and Forecasting

Evaluation measures whether agents reason reliably under realistic uncertainty. Event-forecasting benchmarks and synthetic math-data generation illustrate complementary stress tests for agent planning and reasoning loops.

<!-- FIGURE: evaluation explanatory figure -->

<!-- TABLE: evaluation comparison table -->

<!-- EQUATION: evaluation formal relation -->

<!-- CITATION: ye2024mirai -->

<!-- CITATION: liu2025agenticmath -->
"}, {"chapter_id": "conclusion", "heading": "Conclusion", "body_markdown": "# Conclusion

The manuscript closes by connecting the dimensions into one governed architecture. The practical lesson is that agentic capability depends as much on deterministic boundaries, provenance, and validation as on model fluency.

<!-- FIGURE: conclusion explanatory figure -->

<!-- TABLE: conclusion comparison table -->

<!-- EQUATION: conclusion formal relation -->

<!-- CITATION: ke2025reasoningfrontiers -->

<!-- CITATION: wu2025agenticreasoningtools -->
